questions.objective_ref: Define a vector as an ordered list of scalars, where each position captures a different property of the same thing.
Explain why grouping multiple properties into one vector is more useful than keeping them as separate, unrelated numbers.
Explain why the same position must always mean the same property across all vectors being compared.
Describe how AI systems use feature vectors to represent real-world things so that patterns can be found through arithmetic.
Construct a simple feature vector for a familiar object by deciding which properties to include and assigning a number to each pre-defined position slot.
